# Casey McDougal

> Tennessee-based software engineer leading engineering on a confidential conversational AI product and publishing a free library of dependency-free interface components.

## About

Casey McDougal is a software engineer based in Tennessee. He currently leads engineering on a confidential conversational AI product. The product is intentionally unnamed, and no additional details are public yet.

Casey has shipped software for real businesses, built a Roblox game with returning players, and experimented across web, mobile, games, and AI. The website keeps that history concise rather than presenting every old experiment as a case study.

## Free Component Library

The component library is a free collection of vanilla HTML and CSS interface details. Visitors can preview the components on the homepage and sign up by email for the copy-ready source and usage notes.

The first volume contains:

- **Liquid Glass Button**: A refractive glass pill with a live WebGL chromatic-metal orb and rotating gradient stroke, shipped as a single self-contained JS file with automatic fallbacks.
- **Frosted Navigation**: A responsive sticky navigation bar with readable transparency and a solid fallback when backdrop blur is unavailable.
- **Tactile Glass Card**: A linked or static content surface whose depth makes interaction clear.

- **Glass Toggle**: A springy glass switch with a weighted thumb and an accessible role="switch" pattern.
- **Glass Input**: A sunken glass text field with focus ring and an aria-invalid-driven error state.
- **Glass Dialog**: A frosted modal built on the native dialog element with animated entry and blurred backdrop.

All six components are dependency-free and require no framework or build step.

## Current Focus

Casey is focused on conversational AI, interface engineering, and making software feel natural, capable, and worth trusting. He is open to interesting conversations and unusually good collaborations.
